content_en:
Ibn Qutaibah said, "The meaning is that this is what | have written and counted from his words and deeds. They are all recorded here. The verse includes both meanings. That is to say, this person is the one whom | have been accompanying

* He is Mujahid bin Jabr, Abu Al-Hajaj from Mecca. He is from the followers and is a scholar of recitation and exposition. He learned exegesis from Ibn ‘Abbas, a famous scholar of this nation. He passed away in 104 A.H. See Tahdhib At-Tahdhib, vol.1, p.42, Al-Jarh wat-Ta‘dil, vol.8, p.1469, Mizan AlI'tidal, vol.3, p.439, Lisén Al-Mizén, vol.7, p.349, and Siyar Alam An-Nubala‘,

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 17

and these are his deeds, which | have counted. Then Allah, the Almighty will say,

which means, "(And it will be said), "Both of you throw (Order from Allah to the two angels) into Hell." (Qaf, 50:24)

The order may be referring to the two angels or to the angel that accompanied him during life, and it is the same. The way this speech is written is quite famous among the Arabs. Allah then mentioned six characteristics of the person who would be thrown into Hellfire:

One: He is a disbeliever in Allah, His rights, His religion, Monotheism, His Names, His attributes, His Messengers, and His Angels. He disbelieved in His Books and the Resurrection.

Two: He rejects the truth due to his stubbornness and denial.

Three: He hinders good and that includes both the good that would be done to him if he performed good deeds and the good done to other people. There is no good in him neither for himself nor for his people and this is the state of most creatures.

Four: Besides hindering good to people, he transgresses against man with his hand and his tongue. He is unjust and an oppressor.

Five: He is doubtful, in fact he is suspicious.

Six: Although being a polytheist, who sets up another god with Allah, he worships, loves and hates for His sake, swears by Him, gives oaths to Him, and takes friends and enemies for His sake. The disbeliever might even quarrel with his companions from the devils and Satan would accuse him of wronging himself and that he is the one who pushed himself to transgress. His companion would say that it never had the

18 * AL-FAWA'ID: A Collection of Wise Sayings

power to mislead him but he was himself in error and far astray so he chose his condition for himself. Allah says that Satan would say to the people of Hellfire,

which means, "I had no authority over you except that | called you, SO you responded to me." (Ibrahim, 14:22)

So his companion here is Satan and they are disputing in the presence of Allah.

Another group said, "His companion here is the Angel. He will claim that the angel exaggerated in his writing, was unjust, and that he did not commit all these sins, and that the angel was quick and did not give him a chance to repent before he wrote. The Angel would say that he did not add anything in his writing more than what was _ really done and that he was not quick to write. Allah says that Angel would Say,

which means, "But he was himself in error far astray." (Qaf,

Allah, the Almighty would say,

4B)... GU lpia Y UG >

which means, “Dispute not in front of Me." (Qaf, 50:28)

Allah informed us about the quarrels between the disbelievers and Satan in His presence in chapter of As-Safat and Al-A'raf, the disputes of people in His presence in chapter Az-Zumar, and the disputes of people of Hellfire in chapter Ash-Shu'ara' and Sad.

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 19

After that Allah, the Exalted told us that the Punishment He decrees cannot be changed. This is mentioned in the following verse,

which means, "Surely, | shall fill Hell with jinns and men all together." (Hid, 11:119)

Also His promise to the people of faith to enter Paradise and this will never be changed or reneged. ibn ‘Abbas said, "He wants to say that My promise will never be changed either concerning people who obey Me or those who disobey." Mujahid said, "As if He says, "| have executed what | had decreed." And these are the most correct explanations of this verse."

As Al-Farra' said, "The meaning is that no one will tell lies in the presence of Allah as He knows the unseen." And Ibn Qutaibah said, "It means that sayings will never be distorted, increased, or decreased in Allah’s presence. As he said, "The sayings in My presence and not My sayings." This is, "Lies will never be told in My presence." So from this one point the following two verses bear the same meaning. Allah says,

3 Al-Farra' is Yahya bin Ziyad bin ‘Abdullah bin Mansiir, Abu Zakariyya from Kifah. He is a scholar of grammar and linguistics. They used to say to him, “The emir of believers in grammar." He passed away in 207 A.H. See Tahdhib At-Tahdhib, vol.121, p.212, Al-Ansab, vol.10, p.155, and Tarikh Baghdad,

20 * AL-FAWA'ID: A Collection of Wise Sayings

which means, "And | am not unjust (to the least) to My slaves."

And He, the Exalted says,

That is to say, whatever | said and promised will be done and it will always be just. From the other point, Allah described Himself with two things:

One: sayings would never be changed in Allah's presence as He has perfect knowledge.

Two: He will never be unjust toward His servants as He is perfectly just.

Then He told us about the large space in Hellfire and that whenever people would be thrown inside it, Allah says that it would say,

4D Aue of UA >

which means, "Are there anymore (to come)?" (Qaf, 50:30)

He is wrong who says that this verse indicates the negative, that is to say: that there would be no more to come. Many authentic hadiths refutes this interpretation.

Attributes of the People of Paradise

Allah told us that Paradise will be brought near the pious people and that its peopie have four characteristics:

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 21

One: They are oft-returning to Allah in sincere repentance. These believers always return to Allah after committing sins and start to obey and remember Allah. ‘Ubaid bin 'Umair said, "The repentant is the one who remembers his sins and asks forgiveness for them." Mujahid said, "He is the one who asks forgiveness when he remembers his sins while he is alone. And Sa'id bin Al-Musaiyab* said, "He is the one who commits sin, repents, commits again, and repents again.”

Three: Allah, Exalted says,

“He is Sa'id bin Al-Musaiyab bin Hazan bin Abu Wahab Al-Makhzimy AlQurashy. His surname was Abu Muhammad. He was the head of the followers and one of the seven top jurists in Medina. He used to be called the narrator of 'Umar because he was the best among people to memorize laws under the leadership of ‘Umar bin Al-Khattaéb. He (may Allah be merciful to him) passed away in 94 A.H. See Tahdhib At-Tahdthib, vol.4, p.84, Tarikh AlBukhary Al-Kabir, vol.3, p.510, Al-Jarh wat-Ta‘dil, vol.4, p.262, and Siyar Alam An-Nubala’, vol.4, p.217.

5 He is Qatadah bin Du'amah bin ‘Aziz, Abu Al-Khattab As-Suddsy from Basra. He was an exegete and a memorizer. He was blind and passed away in 118 A.H. See Tahdhib At-Tahdhib, vol.8, p.351, Tarikh Al-Bukhary Al-Kabir, vol.7, p.185, Al-varh wat-Ta’‘dil, vol.7, p.756, Mizan Al-l'tidal, vol.3, p.385, and Lisan Al-Mizan, vol.7, p.341.

which means, "Who feared the Most Beneficent (Allah) in the Ghaib (unseen): (i.e. in this worldly life before seeing and meeting Him)." (Qaf, 50:33)

This verse declares the Existence, Divinity, and Power of Allah and His knowledge of every detail of His servants’ conditions, His Books, Messengers, orders and prohibitions, His promise, threat, and standing before Him on the Day of Resurrection.

Four: Allah, the Almighty says,

which means, “And brought a heart turned in repentance (to Him - and absolutely free from each and every kind of polytheism)." (Qaf, 50:33)

Ibn ‘Abbas said, “He is far from sins and rushes to obey Allah. The reality of repentance is to devote the heart to obedience, love, and striving to be near to Allah.” Allah then mentioned the reward of the one, who has these attributes. Allah says,

ae Cal Gb sl E bem oT aus wh wb STp

which means, "Enter you therein in peace and security; this is a Day of eternal life! There they will have all that they desire, and We have more (for them, i.e. a glance at the Almighty, AllMajestic)." (Qaf, 50:34-35)

Then He frightened them with the destruction that had happened to those who were before them. Such were stronger in power but that did not prevent their destruction. And when the time of destruction came, they ran seeking refuge in the land! Could they find any place of refuge to save themselves from destruction? Qatadah said, "The enemies of Allah got into a fix and found that the order of Allah would catch up with them." Az-Zajjaj said, "They ran seeking refuge but did not find

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 23

any other way except death.” They searched for any other refuge other than death but they did not find any.”

which means, "A reminder for him who has a heart or gives ear while he is heedful." (Qaf, 50:37)

He then told us that He created the heavens and the earth and all between them in six Days and that nothing of fatigue touched Him. He accused His enemies from among the Jews of lying when they said that Allah rested on the seventh day. Then He ordered His Prophet to follow His example and be patient toward what his enemies are saying about him, as Allah was patient toward what the Jews had said about Him. And there is no one more capable of being patient than Him. He ordered him to do something that will help him to be patient, and that is to glorify the Praises of His Lord, before the rising of the sun and before its setting (i.e. the Fajr, Zhuhr, and 'Asr prayers), as well as during a part of the night also, glorify His praises (i.e. Maghrib and ‘Isha’ prayers). Likewise there are more prayers other than the Fard prayers: (As-Sunnah, Nawéafil optional and additional prayers). It was said that after the /sha prayer is Al-Witr prayer (Odd prayer) and said it means the two rak'ahs after Maghrib (Sunset Prayer). The first is the opinion of Ibn ‘Abbas and the other is of 'Umar, ‘Aly, Abu Hurairah, and AlHasan bin ‘Aly. And there is a third opinion from Ibn ‘Abbas that it means glorifying Allah verbally after every obligatory prayer.
content_id:
Ibn Qutaibah berkata, "Maknanya adalah inilah yang telah aku tulis dan catat dari ucapan dan perbuatannya. Semuanya tercatat di sini. Ayat tersebut mencakup kedua makna itu. Artinya, orang ini adalah orang yang telah aku dampingi

* Beliau adalah Mujahid bin Jabr, Abu Al-Hajaj dari Makkah. Beliau termasuk golongan tabi'in dan merupakan ulama dalam bidang qira'at dan tafsir. Beliau mempelajari tafsir dari Ibn 'Abbas, ulama terkenal umat ini. Beliau wafat pada tahun 104 H. Lihat Tahdzib At-Tahdzib, jil.1, hal.42, Al-Jarh wat-Ta'dil, jil.8, hal.1469, Mizan Al-I'tidal, jil.3, hal.439, Lisan Al-Mizan, jil.7, hal.349, dan Siyar Alam An-Nubala'.

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Sayings * 17

dan inilah amal perbuatannya, yang telah aku catat. Kemudian Allah Yang Mahakuasa akan berfirman,

yang berarti, "(Dan akan dikatakan), 'Keduanya campakkanlah (perintah dari Allah kepada dua malaikat) ke dalam Neraka.'" (Qaf, 50:24)

Perintah itu mungkin merujuk kepada dua malaikat atau kepada malaikat yang mendampinginya semasa hidup, dan maknanya sama. Gaya penulisan perkataan ini cukup terkenal di kalangan bangsa Arab. Allah kemudian menyebutkan enam ciri orang yang akan dilempar ke dalam Neraka:

Pertama: Ia adalah orang kafir kepada Allah, hak-hak-Nya, agama-Nya, tauhid, Asma'-Nya, sifat-sifat-Nya, para Rasul-Nya, dan para Malaikat-Nya. Ia mengingkari Kitab-kitab-Nya dan Kebangkitan.

Kedua: Ia menolak kebenaran karena keras kepala dan pengingkaran.

Ketiga: Ia menghalangi kebaikan, dan itu mencakup kebaikan yang akan diperolehnya jika ia berbuat baik maupun kebaikan bagi orang lain. Tidak ada kebaikan dalam dirinya, tidak untuk dirinya sendiri maupun untuk kaumnya, dan inilah kondisi kebanyakan makhluk.

Keempat: Selain menghalangi kebaikan bagi orang lain, ia pun berbuat zalim terhadap manusia dengan tangan dan lisannya. Ia adalah orang yang tidak adil dan seorang penindas.

Kelima: Ia penuh keraguan, bahkan penuh dengan prasangka.

Keenam: Meskipun ia seorang musyrik yang menyekutukan Allah dengan tuhan lain, ia tetap beribadah, mencintai dan membenci karena-Nya, bersumpah dengan-Nya, berikrar kepada-Nya, serta mengambil kawan dan musuh demi-Nya. Orang kafir itu bahkan mungkin bertengkar dengan teman-temannya dari golongan setan, dan setan pun menuduhnya telah menzalimi dirinya sendiri dan bahwa dialah yang mendorong dirinya untuk berbuat aniaya. Teman setan itu berkata bahwa ia tidak pernah memiliki

18 * AL-FAWA'ID: A Collection of Wise Sayings

kekuatan untuk menyesatkannya, namun orang itu sendirilah yang berada dalam kesesatan dan sangat jauh menyimpang sehingga ia memilih kondisi itu bagi dirinya sendiri. Allah berfirman bahwa setan akan berkata kepada penghuni Neraka,

yang berarti, "Aku tidak mempunyai kekuasaan apa pun atas kalian, kecuali aku menyeru kalian, lalu kalian memenuhi seruanku." (Ibrahim, 14:22)

Jadi temannya di sini adalah setan dan mereka saling berbantahan di hadapan Allah.

Kelompok lain berkata, "Temannya di sini adalah Malaikat. Ia akan mengklaim bahwa malaikat berlebih-lebihan dalam penulisannya, berbuat zalim, dan bahwa ia tidak melakukan semua dosa ini, serta bahwa malaikat terburu-buru dan tidak memberinya kesempatan untuk bertobat sebelum menulis. Malaikat akan berkata bahwa ia tidak menambahkan apa pun dalam tulisannya melebihi apa yang benar-benar dilakukan dan bahwa ia tidak tergesa-gesa dalam menulis. Allah berfirman bahwa Malaikat akan berkata,

yang berarti, "Tetapi dia sendiri yang berada dalam kesesatan yang jauh." (Qaf,

Allah Yang Mahakuasa akan berfirman,

4B)... GU lpia Y UG >

yang berarti, "Janganlah kamu bertengkar di hadapan-Ku." (Qaf, 50:28)

Allah memberitahu kita tentang pertengkaran antara orang-orang kafir dan setan di hadapan-Nya dalam surah As-Saffat dan Al-A'raf, pertengkaran manusia di hadapan-Nya dalam surah Az-Zumar, serta pertengkaran penghuni Neraka dalam surah Asy-Syu'ara' dan Sad.

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Sayings * 19

Setelah itu Allah Yang Maha Tinggi memberitahu kita bahwa hukuman yang Ia tetapkan tidak dapat diubah. Hal ini disebutkan dalam ayat berikut,

yang berarti, "Sesungguhnya Aku pasti akan memenuhi Neraka Jahannam dengan jin dan manusia semuanya." (Hud, 11:119)

Demikian pula janji-Nya kepada orang-orang beriman untuk masuk Surga dan ini tidak akan pernah diubah atau dibatalkan. Ibn 'Abbas berkata, "Ia ingin mengatakan bahwa janji-Ku tidak akan pernah berubah, baik terhadap orang-orang yang menaati-Ku maupun yang mendurhakai-Ku." Mujahid berkata, "Seolah-olah Dia berfirman, 'Aku telah melaksanakan apa yang telah Aku tetapkan.' Dan inilah penafsiran yang paling tepat untuk ayat ini."

Sebagaimana Al-Farra' berkata, "Maknanya adalah bahwa tidak ada seorang pun yang akan berdusta di hadapan Allah karena Dia mengetahui yang gaib." Dan Ibn Qutaibah berkata, "Artinya ucapan-ucapan tidak akan pernah diputarbalikkan, ditambah, atau dikurangi di hadapan Allah. Sebagaimana Dia berfirman, 'Ucapan-ucapan di hadapan-Ku dan bukan ucapan-Ku.' Ini berarti, 'Kebohongan tidak akan pernah diucapkan di hadapan-Ku.' Jadi dari satu sudut pandang ini, dua ayat berikut memiliki makna yang sama. Allah berfirman,

3 Al-Farra' adalah Yahya bin Ziyad bin 'Abdullah bin Manshur, Abu Zakariyya dari Kufah. Beliau adalah ulama nahwu dan linguistik. Orang-orang biasa menyebutnya, "Amirul mukminin dalam bidang nahwu." Beliau wafat pada tahun 207 H. Lihat Tahdzib At-Tahdzib, jil.121, hal.212, Al-Ansab, jil.10, hal.155, dan Tarikh Baghdad.

20 * AL-FAWA'ID: A Collection of Wise Sayings

yang berarti, "Dan Aku sekali-kali tidak berbuat zalim kepada hamba-hamba-Ku."

Dan Dia Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman,

Artinya, apa pun yang Aku ucapkan dan janjikan pasti akan terlaksana dan senantiasa adil. Dari sudut pandang lain, Allah menggambarkan diri-Nya dengan dua hal:

Pertama: Ucapan-ucapan tidak akan pernah diubah di hadapan Allah karena Dia memiliki ilmu yang sempurna.

Kedua: Dia tidak akan pernah berlaku zalim kepada hamba-hamba-Nya karena Dia Maha Adil.

Kemudian Dia memberitahu kita tentang luasnya ruang dalam Neraka dan bahwa setiap kali orang-orang dilempar ke dalamnya, Allah berfirman bahwa Neraka itu akan berkata,

4D Aue of UA >

yang berarti, "Apakah masih ada lagi (yang akan datang)?" (Qaf, 50:30)

Ia keliru yang berpendapat bahwa ayat ini menunjukkan makna negatif, yaitu bahwa tidak ada lagi yang akan datang. Banyak hadith shahih yang membantah penafsiran ini.

Sifat-Sifat Penghuni Surga

Allah memberitahu kita bahwa Surga akan didekatkan kepada orang-orang yang bertaqwa dan bahwa penghuninya memiliki empat ciri:

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Sayings * 21

Pertama: Mereka adalah orang-orang yang senantiasa kembali kepada Allah dengan taubat yang tulus. Para mukmin ini selalu kembali kepada Allah setelah melakukan dosa dan mulai menaati serta berdzikir kepada Allah. 'Ubaid bin 'Umair berkata, "Orang yang bertaubat adalah orang yang mengingat dosa-dosanya dan memohon ampunan atasnya." Mujahid berkata, "Ia adalah orang yang memohon ampunan ketika mengingat dosa-dosanya saat ia sendirian." Dan Sa'id bin Al-Musaiyab* berkata, "Ia adalah orang yang berbuat dosa, bertaubat, berbuat dosa lagi, dan bertaubat lagi."

Ketiga: Allah Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman,

"Beliau adalah Sa'id bin Al-Musaiyab bin Hazan bin Abu Wahab Al-Makhzumy Al-Qurashy. Kunyahnya adalah Abu Muhammad. Beliau adalah pemimpin tabi'in dan salah satu dari tujuh fuqaha terkemuka di Madinah. Beliau biasa disebut sebagai perawi 'Umar karena beliaulah yang paling baik dalam menghafal hukum-hukum di bawah kepemimpinan 'Umar bin Al-Khattab. Beliau (semoga Allah merahmatinya) wafat pada tahun 94 H. Lihat Tahdzib At-Tahdzib, jil.4, hal.84, Tarikh Al-Bukhari Al-Kabir, jil.3, hal.510, Al-Jarh wat-Ta'dil, jil.4, hal.262, dan Siyar Alam An-Nubala', jil.4, hal.217.

5 Beliau adalah Qatadah bin Du'amah bin 'Aziz, Abu Al-Khattab As-Suddusy dari Basra. Beliau adalah seorang ahli tafsir dan hafizh. Beliau buta dan wafat pada tahun 118 H. Lihat Tahdzib At-Tahdzib, jil.8, hal.351, Tarikh Al-Bukhari Al-Kabir, jil.7, hal.185, Al-Jarh wat-Ta'dil, jil.7, hal.756, Mizan Al-I'tidal, jil.3, hal.385, dan Lisan Al-Mizan, jil.7, hal.341.

yang berarti, "Yang takut kepada Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah dalam keadaan yang gaib (yaitu dalam kehidupan dunia ini sebelum melihat dan berjumpa dengan-Nya)." (Qaf, 50:33)

Ayat ini menyatakan Keberadaan, Ketuhanan, dan Kekuasaan Allah serta pengetahuan-Nya tentang setiap perincian kondisi hamba-hamba-Nya, Kitab-kitab-Nya, para Rasul-Nya, perintah dan larangan-Nya, janji dan ancaman-Nya, serta berdiri di hadapan-Nya pada Hari Kebangkitan.

Keempat: Allah Yang Mahakuasa berfirman,

yang berarti, "Dan datang dengan hati yang bertaubat (kepada-Nya — dan benar-benar bebas dari setiap jenis syirik)." (Qaf, 50:33)

Ibn 'Abbas berkata, "Ia jauh dari dosa-dosa dan bersegera untuk menaati Allah. Hakikat taubat adalah mengabdikan hati kepada ketaatan, kecintaan, dan berusaha untuk mendekat kepada Allah." Allah kemudian menyebutkan pahala bagi orang yang memiliki sifat-sifat ini. Allah berfirman,

ae Cal Gb sl E bem oT aus wh wb STp

yang berarti, "Masuklah ke dalamnya dengan sejahtera dan aman; inilah Hari kehidupan yang kekal! Di sana mereka mendapat apa yang mereka kehendaki, dan Kami mempunyai tambahan (bagi mereka, yaitu memandang Allah Yang Mahagung lagi Maha Mulia)." (Qaf, 50:34-35)

Kemudian Dia menakut-nakuti mereka dengan kebinasaan yang telah menimpa orang-orang sebelum mereka. Mereka lebih kuat dalam kekuasaan tetapi hal itu tidak mencegah kebinasaan mereka. Dan ketika saat kebinasaan tiba, mereka berlari mencari perlindungan di bumi! Dapatkah mereka menemukan tempat perlindungan untuk menyelamatkan diri dari kebinasaan? Qatadah berkata, "Para musuh Allah terjebak dalam kesulitan dan mendapati bahwa ketetapan Allah akan mengejar mereka." Az-Zajjaj berkata, "Mereka berlari mencari perlindungan namun tidak menemukan

AL-FAWA'ID : Kumpulan Ucapan-Ucapan Bijak * 23

jalan lain kecuali kematian." Mereka mencari tempat perlindungan lain selain kematian namun tidak mereka temukan."

yang bermakna, "Sebagai peringatan bagi orang yang memiliki hati atau memberikan pendengaran sedang dia menyaksikan." (Qaf, 50:37)

Kemudian Allah memberitahu kita bahwa Dia menciptakan langit dan bumi serta segala yang ada di antara keduanya dalam enam Hari dan tidak ada kelelahan yang menyentuh-Nya. Dia mendustakan musuh-musuh-Nya dari kalangan Yahudi yang mengatakan bahwa Allah beristirahat pada hari ketujuh. Kemudian Dia memerintahkan Nabi-Nya untuk meneladani-Nya dan bersabar terhadap apa yang dikatakan musuh-musuhnya tentang beliau, sebagaimana Allah bersabar terhadap apa yang dikatakan orang-orang Yahudi tentang-Nya. Dan tidak ada seorang pun yang lebih mampu bersabar daripada-Nya. Dia memerintahkan beliau untuk melakukan sesuatu yang akan membantunya bersabar, yaitu bertasbih memuji Tuhannya, sebelum terbit matahari dan sebelum terbenamnya (yakni salah Fajr, Zuhur, dan Ashar), serta di sebagian malam juga bertasbih memuji-Nya (yakni salah Maghrib dan Isya'). Demikian pula terdapat salah-salah lain selain salah Fardhu: (As-Sunnah, Nawafil yaitu salah-salah sunnah dan tambahan). Dikatakan bahwa setelah salah Isya' adalah salah Al-Witr (salah Ganjil) dan ada yang mengatakan maknanya adalah dua raka'at setelah Maghrib (Salah Maghrib). Yang pertama adalah pendapat Ibn 'Abbas dan yang lain adalah pendapat 'Umar, 'Ali, Abu Hurairah, dan Al-Hasan bin 'Ali. Dan ada pendapat ketiga dari Ibn 'Abbas bahwa maknanya adalah bertasbih kepada Allah secara lisan setelah setiap salah wajib.
content_ar: Ibn Qutaibah said, "The meaning is that this is what | have written and counted from his words and deeds. They are all recorded here. The الآية includes both meanings. بمعنى, this person is the one whom | have been accompanying * He is Mujahid bin Jabr, Abu Al-Hajaj from مكة المكرمة. He is from the followers and is a scholar of التلاوة and exposition. He learned exegesis from Ibn ‘Abbas, a famous scholar of this nation. He passed away in 104 A.H. See Tahdhib At-Tahdhib, vol.1, p.42, Al-Jarh wat-Ta‘dil, vol.8, p.1469, الميزان علي'tidal, vol.3, p.439, Lisén Al-Mizén, vol.7, p.349, and Siyar Alam An-Nubala‘, AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 17 and these are his deeds, which | have counted. Then الله, the Almighty will say, أي, "(And it will be said), "Both of you throw (Order from الله to the two الملائكة) into النار." (Qaf, 50:24) The order may be referring to the two الملائكة or to the angel that accompanied him خلال الحياة, and it is the same. The الطريق this speech is written is quite famous among the Arabs. الله then mentioned six characteristics of the person who would be thrown into النار: One: He is a disbeliever in الله, His الحقوق, His religion, Monotheism, His Names, His attributes, His الرسل, and His الملائكة. He disbelieved in His الكتب and the البعث. Two: He rejects the الحق بسبب his stubbornness and denial. Three: He hinders الخير and that includes both the الخير that would be done to him إذا he performed الخير deeds and the الخير done to other people. There is no الخير in him neither for himself nor for his people and this is the state of most creatures. Four: بالإضافة إلى hindering الخير to people, he transgresses against man with his hand and his tongue. He is unjust and an oppressor. Five: He is doubtful, in fact he is suspicious. Six: وإن كان being a polytheist, who sets up another god with الله, he worships, loves and hates for His sake, swears by Him, gives oaths to Him, and takes friends and enemies for His sake. The disbeliever might even quarrel with his الصحابة from the devils and الشيطان would accuse him of wronging himself and that he is the one who pushed himself to transgress. His companion would say that it never had the 18 * AL-FAWA'ID: A Collection of Wise Sayings power to mislead him but he was himself in error and far astray لذا he chose his condition for himself. الله says that الشيطان would say to the people of النار, أي, "I had no authority over you except that | called you, لذا you responded to me." (إبراهيم ﷺ, 14:22) لذا his companion here is الشيطان and they are disputing in the presence of الله. Another group said, "His companion here is the Angel. He will claim that the angel exaggerated in his writing, was unjust, and that he did not commit all these الذنوب, and that the angel was quick and did not give him a chance to repent قبل كتب.
